comments.body: That may work or not, depending which process is used to start the cmd.exe instance. If you use Windows explorer which recognizes the ENV changes and updates them internally then the changes will also be present in the cmd child porocess. However is a process is used as parent process which ignores the env changes then cmd will also get the old values. I had this case once on a system which used OpenShell as start menu replacement and OpenShell ignored env changes...

answers.body: If you open a new cmd window it should use the updated environment variables.

title: After installing Java 8, I configured environment variables. How can I make them work without restarting Windows?
body:
I have set the environment variables correctly，

I also switched the Java environment variables version to 17, but the current system running cmd is still a Java 1.8 version. I need to let the environment variables take effect without starting the system.
